metadata.summary: Versioned React component library with semver discipline, design tokens, and Radix UI primitives. Packaged library distinct from shadcn's copy-paste model with consistent prop API.

content.title: UI Component Library
content.summary: Versioned, semver-controlled React component library with a layered structure (primitives → composite → patterns → layout), consistent prop API doctrine, design token integration, co-located Storybook stories + tests, and Radix UI/React Aria for interactive primitives. Distinct from the shadcn copy-paste model — this methodology produces a packaged library.

why.p: A shared library enforces API consistency, accessibility, and token usage across multiple apps in a monorepo. Without a library, each app reimplements the same Button/Modal/Input with slight API differences, creating inconsistency and compounding accessibility debt. Co-locating tests and stories with each component ensures regressions are caught immediately.

list.item: Multi-app monorepo that needs shared, versioned UI components (web, admin, mobile-web)
Enforcing accessibility, theming, and prop API consistency via code review and audits
Need semver-controlled exports consumed by multiple packages
Single small app with no plans to share components — premature extraction adds overhead
Heavy bespoke-per-page styling with low reuse (marketing landing pages, one-off campaigns)
Replacing well-maintained external libraries (MUI, Mantine) without a clear extension story
Team cannot commit to maintaining stories, tests, and tokens — the library will rot

rule.statement: Use the same prop names for the same concept across all components: `variant`, `size`, `isLoading`, `leftIcon`, `rightIcon`, `asChild`, `fullWidth`. Never invent synonyms (`tone`, `colorScheme`, `appearance`) for the same concept.
rule.rationale: Inconsistent naming forces consumers to remember different prop names per component; API drift compounds as the library grows.

rule.statement: Ban layout props (`marginBottom`, `paddingX`) from primitive components. Layout belongs on layout components (Stack, Box, Grid).
rule.rationale: Layout props on primitives couple the component to usage context and make it impossible to use the same component in different spacing contexts.

rule.statement: Lock the public API: barrel exports only. Never `import { foo } from '@org/ui/dist/internal/...'`.
rule.rationale: Internal paths are not semver-stable; consumers importing them break silently on any refactor.

rule.statement: All colors, radii, and spacing in components must reference design tokens (CSS variables or Style Dictionary output). No literal values in component code.
rule.rationale: Token-based styling means theme changes reach all components via a single token update, with no component code changes required.

rule.statement: Set `sideEffects: false` in `package.json` and use ESM exports to enable tree-shaking. Every consumer pays only for what they import.
rule.rationale: Without tree-shaking metadata, a single import of any component pulls in the entire library bundle.

rule.statement: Use Radix UI or React Aria for every interactive primitive that requires focus traps, roving tabindex, or ARIA patterns (dropdown, dialog, combobox, tree). Never hand-roll these behaviors.
rule.rationale: Custom implementations of composite ARIA widgets are notoriously incomplete; Radix and React Aria implement the full WAI-ARIA Authoring Practices patterns correctly.

rule.statement: Add axe-core assertions to every component test. Accessibility failures block CI, not a separate audit queue.
rule.rationale: Deferred accessibility audits are never prioritized; inline testing catches regressions at the PR boundary.

rule.statement: Any change to `src/index.ts` (the public barrel) is a semver event. Require human approval and a changesets entry before merging.
rule.rationale: Silent API changes break dependent apps silently; human review + changesets creates a changelog trail for consumers.

rule.statement: Maintain a `/migrations.md` document that agents append to whenever they break a public API.
rule.rationale: Without a migrations guide, consumers cannot upgrade; the library becomes a maintenance burden instead of a productivity asset.

antipattern.reason: Libraries must declare `react` as a peer dependency; listing it as a regular dependency forces duplicate React instances in consumers, causing hook errors.

antipattern.description: Adding `:global(.foo)` CSS selector inside CSS Modules.
antipattern.reason: Global selectors leak out of the component and affect elements elsewhere in the host app unpredictably.

example.title: Button component implementation
example.note: forwardRef + displayName. asChild uses Radix Slot. Decorative icons are aria-hidden. isLoading disables to prevent double-submit.

example.title: Input component with ARIA
example.note: useId for stable IDs. aria-invalid + aria-describedby wired to error/helper. role="alert" on error announces to screen readers.

section.name: agentic-workflow
section.p: Use a subagent to (1) draft the component spec (props, variants, a11y notes) before writing code, (2) implement primitive + tests + Storybook story atomically, (3) run a11y + visual regression checks, (4) update the public barrel. Library work is naturally three-phase — spec → impl → review — so route each phase to a different agent role with clear handoffs. Keep the design-token JSON outside any single component to allow theme edits without touching component source.

list.item: component-architect (Sonnet) — proposes API surface, variant set, a11y model from a one-line brief.
component-implementer (Sonnet/Haiku) — writes the `.tsx` + CSS + Story + test scaffold.
a11y-auditor (Haiku) — runs axe-core, keyboard tab tests, ARIA attribute review.
bundle-watcher (Haiku) — runs `size-limit` / `bundlewatch` and refuses PRs that bloat exports.
storybook-story-writer (Haiku) — generates stories covering all variants × states (loading, disabled, error).
Lock the public API: barrel exports only — never `import { foo } from '@org/ui/dist/internal/...'`.
Use design tokens (CSS variables or Style Dictionary) for all colors, radii, spacing — components reference tokens, never literals.
Co-locate test + story + source per component; agents touching one usually need to touch all three.
Define a "prop-API doctrine": same name = same meaning across components (`variant`, `size`, `isLoading`, `leftIcon`, `rightIcon`, `asChild`, `fullWidth`).
Ban prop names that imply layout in primitives (`marginBottom`, `paddingX`) — those belong on layout components (Stack, Box).
Add a11y assertions to every test (axe-core) — not optional.
Maintain an `/migrations.md` doc that agents append to whenever they break a public API.
Use `peerDependencies` for `react`, `react-dom`, never `dependencies`.
Agents reinvent primitives (custom dropdown menu) instead of wrapping Radix; require a "use Radix or React Aria" rule for interactive primitives.
API drift: agents add `colorScheme` here, `tone` there. Enforce with a static check that all components expose the same `variant` enum names where applicable.
"Helpful" defaults: agents set defaults that hide bugs (`isLoading={false}` is fine; `onClose={() => {}}` masks missing wiring).
Storybook control mismatch: agents forget `argTypes`, leaving controls broken — require lint rule that every prop has a control or `control: { disable: true }`.
Forgetting `"use client"` (Next.js) or `import 'client-only'` — components break SSR silently.
Treating CSS modules as global: agents add `:global(.foo)` selectors that leak. Hook lints for `:global` outside specific files.
Human-in-loop checkpoint: any change to public barrel (`src/index.ts`) is a semver event — require human approval and a changeset.

template.purpose: Scaffold component folder with tsx, css, stories, barrel update
